title: Vendor Intake Security Policy
body:
## Purpose
This policy defines the security criteria and compliance checks required for onboarding new vendors. It ensures third-party vendors meet our organization's security standards before accessing systems or data.

## Scope
Applies to all vendors, suppliers, and partners that will process, store, or transmit company data or connect to our networks.

## Required Security Certifications and Standards
Vendors must provide evidence of the following:
- SOC 2 Type II report (current within 12 months) for data handling vendors.
- ISO 27001:2022 certification for services involving personal or sensitive data.
- PCI DSS compliance if handling payment card data.
- HIPAA compliance if handling protected health information (PHI).
- CSA STAR Level 2 or higher for cloud services.

## Technical Security Controls
1. Data Encryption: All data at rest must be encrypted using AES-256 or higher. Data in transit must use TLS 1.3.
2. Access Control: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) must be enforced for all user accounts with access to company resources.
3. Logging and Monitoring: Centralized logging with real-time alerting for security events; logs retained for minimum 90 days.
4. Vulnerability Management: Quarterly external vulnerability scans and annual penetration tests by an accredited third party.
5. Incident Response: Documented incident response plan tested annually; notification within 24 hours of confirmed breach.
6. Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery: RPO of 4 hours and RTO of 8 hours for critical services; annual DR test.

## Data Privacy and Governance
- Data classification and handling in line with company data classification policy.
- Right-to-audit clause allowing annual security audits.
- Data deletion and retention policies aligned with legal requirements.
- Sub-processor management: vendor must notify us before engaging new sub-processors.

## Compliance Checks During Intake
1. Collect vendor security questionnaire (SIG Lite or CAIQ).
2. Review security documentation: policies, certificates, pen test reports.
3. Evaluate against risk appetite: score based on data sensitivity, access level, and criticality.
4. Perform on-site audit for high-risk vendors.
5. Obtain approval from Security, Legal, and Procurement before granting access.

## Review Cadence
- Annual re-assessment for critical vendors.
- Re-assessment upon significant change in vendor service or security posture.

## Non-Compliance
If a vendor fails to meet required criteria, a risk acceptance must be signed by CISO or equivalent. Remediation plan with timelines must be documented.

title: General Vendor Management Overview
body: This article provides an overview of the vendor lifecycle from sourcing to offboarding. It does not detail security requirements, which are covered in KB-5001.

title: How to Classify Vendor Data Sensitivity
body: Guidelines for classifying the sensitivity of data that vendors may access. Refer to KB-5001 for specific security controls required per data classification level.
